rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the FLAW at data = dataBadBuffer (around line 33) and explained that the loop uses strlen(dest) to read up to 99 bytes from a 50-byte source, causing over-read at dest[i] = data[i].
rationale.local_fix: They proposed bounding the read by using destLen = strlen(data) and iterating to that bound, which prevents reading past the source buffer.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended passing explicit lengths via safer abstractions (e.g., a struct pairing buffer and size or string_view-like types) and using size-aware operations, which eliminates reliance on sentinel-terminated raw buffers.
